disclaimer: Indicator formulas and thresholds are illustrative defaults. Owners must establish baselines, sampling, decision rights and context-specific thresholds before operational use.

indicators.name: Material data provenance coverage
indicators.purpose: Track whether material data inputs have reviewed provenance records.
indicators.formula: reviewed material data sources / identified material data sources * 100
indicators.caveat: Coverage measures documentation presence, not correctness, permission, or fitness for use.

indicators.name: Required review completion
indicators.purpose: Track whether decisions requiring human review received a recorded review before execution.
indicators.formula: eligible decisions with timely recorded review / eligible decisions * 100
indicators.caveat: Completion alone does not show that review was informed, independent, or able to alter the outcome.

indicators.name: Supplier evidence coverage
indicators.purpose: Track completion of required supplier evidence items for in-scope services.
indicators.formula: accepted required supplier evidence items / required supplier evidence items * 100
indicators.caveat: Accepted evidence must still be tested for relevance, recency, scope and independent support.

indicators.name: Median incident triage time
indicators.purpose: Track elapsed time from detection to accountable initial classification and containment assignment.
indicators.formula: median minutes from detectedAt to triagedAt for in-scope incidents
indicators.caveat: Fast triage is not equivalent to correct severity, containment quality, or regulatory reporting.

indicators.name: Ungated material scope changes
indicators.purpose: Detect purpose, population, authority or workflow changes that bypassed the change gate.
indicators.formula: ungated material changes / identified material changes * 100
indicators.caveat: A useful denominator depends on reliable change detection and an agreed definition of material change.

indicators.name: Sensitive-data control events
indicators.purpose: Surface unauthorized access, disclosure, retention or use involving sensitive data.
indicators.formula: count of confirmed in-scope sensitive-data control events

thresholds.red: 2 or more events or any severe event

indicators.caveat: Counts depend on detection quality and do not by themselves determine legal incident materiality.

indicators.name: Human override failure rate
indicators.purpose: Detect cases where a required reviewer could not pause, alter or reverse a system action.
indicators.formula: failed override attempts / recorded override attempts * 100
indicators.caveat: Low rates can hide low usage, poor logging or reviewers avoiding an ineffective mechanism.

indicators.name: Critical adversarial test failure rate
indicators.purpose: Track unresolved critical failures in approved misuse and failure scenarios.
indicators.formula: unresolved critical failed tests / executed critical tests * 100
indicators.cadence: per release and monthly for adaptive systems

thresholds.red: any failure without an approved containment decision

indicators.caveat: The metric is only as useful as scenario coverage, test independence and severity calibration.

indicators.name: Tool call error rate
indicators.purpose: Track the rate of malformed, hallucinated, or unauthorized tool calls made by autonomous agents.
indicators.formula: unauthorized or failed tool calls / total tool calls * 100

thresholds.red: above 5% or any unauthorized write attempt

indicators.caveat: High error rates indicate semantic drift, poor schema alignment, or attempt to bypass controls.

indicators.name: Multi-agent conflict rate
indicators.purpose: Track deadlocked, conflicting, or terminated multi-agent workflows.
indicators.formula: deadlocked or failed workflow runs / total multi-agent runs * 100
indicators.caveat: Signals goal conflicts, prompt mismatches, or infinite message loop issues.
